behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Unit. Re-review: the real failure is at the command→artifact-store boundary (wrong slot written), not pure local logic. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, and correctness does not depend on model output quality. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is only about artifact destination: "stores grounding into ... `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact."
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage with an in-process run context + artifact repository, then assert `grounding` is written and `plan` is unchanged by that stage.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Integration. Re-review against Agentic: disqualified because behavior text targets IO/orchestration semantics (read one artifact, write another), not whether LLM content is good. No OS/network/subprocess boundary is required. Rejected Workflow because this behavior can be validated in a single implement-stage execution with pre-seeded grounding.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a run context with a `grounding` artifact, execute implement-stage with deterministic fixtures, then assert grounding was consumed (or referenced) and a new `plan` artifact was emitted without overwriting grounding.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Unit. Re-review: failure appears when deep-dive stage interacts with existing issue content plus artifact persistence (side effect suppression), which is a module handoff/state interaction. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: "no longer overwrites `plan`" is about write-side effect control, not real infra or model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content that already contains diagnosis, run deep-dive stage with real stage wiring and artifact store, and assert no write/update occurs to `plan`.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Integration. Re-review confirms boundary is cross-module state lineage in one run context (producer stage -> storage -> consumer stage). No requirement for real OS/network/subprocess. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text is lineage/readability, not model decision correctness: "implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier."
behaviors.test_description: Execute producer stage that writes `grounding`, persist run context, then execute implement-stage in the same context and assert it resolves the exact prior grounding artifact by lineage metadata/id.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Agentic. Re-review upgrades to Workflow because behavior text explicitly requires a full multi-step chain and end-to-end carryover across multiple agentic stages. Integration is disqualified by explicit sequence requirement: "full multi-step agent run"; single-step or stubbed orchestration can miss cross-step degradation.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real chained pipeline (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`) in one run context and assert the final `plan` includes concrete elements traceable to earlier `grounding` content, validating cross-step propagation.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Unit. Re-review: fallback safety plus non-corruption is a state interaction across implement flow and artifact store, so Integration is the minimum realistic boundary. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text because this is deterministic fallback behavior with absent input, not real infra or model-quality dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Start with run context lacking `grounding` but containing other artifacts, run implement-stage, assert a valid `plan` is produced, and verify pre-existing artifacts remain unchanged.
